rag_rules: Preserve AADL structure by placing contract content only in scopes where the referenced symbols are visible.
Keep component-type contracts separate from implementation-level bindings; implementation content belongs in implementation scope.
Do not introduce references across owner boundaries unless the model exposes them through valid features or locally declared names.
generic_rules: Insert new annex content without altering existing component, implementation, subcomponent, connection, or property structure.
When merging generated content, preserve original ordering and surrounding architecture text; add only the minimal scoped annex material.
Avoid using implementation-only paths in type-owned contract content, and avoid type-only assumptions about implementation internals.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is enclosed in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-level bindings belong after relevant structural declarations in the owning implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not move clauses between component type and implementation just to satisfy a case outcome.
Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were in AGREE expression scope unless explicitly exposed by the model.
irrelevant_topics: Case-specific threshold values or function arguments
